summary:
Carbon steel for hand instruments typically contains around 0.6-0.8% carbon to ensure durability during high-temperature sterilization processes. This percentage supports its use in tools subject to frequent autoclaving at 121°C to 134°C.
The summary focuses only on the critical information regarding the specific range of carbon concentration necessary for maintaining instrument integrity through required sterilization procedures. It omits extraneous details about tool call actions or temperature ranges not directly related to answering the core query.
